opp.name: Eversana OppS2C - Mar'25
opp.next_step: 10/13/25 RH Invite Chamika to PLaN- determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled.

items.quote: our CFO has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low
Curious if you have dropped the initiative completely...

items.source: Avoma call 28 May 2025 (only informal criteria surfaced)

metrics.narrative: On the 28 May 2025 call, Jeannine Jenkins quantified the current AP tool's failure directly: 'we implemented Xflow, they said you we would get about an 80% match. So two years later, we're still at 5% match.' She also cited '30 to 45, 50 requests a day' hitting the vendor-inquiry inbox and invoices requiring '4 or 5 times' manual touch when PO funds run short, plus 'our CFO has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low.' This is a strong, quantified pain case even though it has not been reconfirmed since.

sources: SF Task records 05 Jun 2025 - 15 Jan 2026 (Chamika McLean)
champion.narrative: Chamika McLean was an actively developing champion early on: she requested the recording and deck, committed to 'converse with additional individuals in her organization,' and did bring Jeannine Jenkins onto the 28 May 2025 call. On 5 Jun 2025 she replied to a scheduling email promising she'd 'definitely like to schedule a workshop/demo' and would check Jeannine's calendar. What is missing: since that 5 Jun 2025 reply she has not responded to at least six dated follow-ups (20 Jun 2025, 28 Jul 2025, 4 Aug 2025, 13 Oct 2025, 15 Jan 2026), a 13-month cooling period with zero reply.

competition.narrative: No rival vendor is named anywhere: Competitors__c is blank and neither Avoma call references a competing bidder. Eversana is dissatisfied with its current AP tool Xflow (a Microsoft Dynamics D365 F&O module) and said on 28 May 2025 it is 'beginning the process of seeing what other options are out there,' but has not named any other vendor being evaluated alongside Zycus. The real threat for this deal is inertia: the rep's own 13 Oct 2025 next step asks whether the initiative has been 'dropped completely,' which remains unanswered.

sources: Avoma call 28 May 2025 (Chamika McLean, Jeannine Jenkins)
identify_pain.narrative: Both Chamika McLean and Jeannine Jenkins detailed extensive operational pain on 28 May 2025: manual PO-fund lookups causing invoices to be 'touched at least 4 times,' a CAO who 'spends significant hours weekly' manually splitting invoices like a $50,000 PWC bill across departments because the system 'does not effectively accommodate allocation adjustments,' and unapplied cash from remittance-format mismatches. Pain owners are the AP team (Chamika McLean) and finance leadership (Jeannine Jenkins, and CFO Kelly Baker by reference).

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, MSA, or procurement-process discussion has ever occurred; the deal has never progressed past initial discovery. This is genuinely too early to assess, not a hidden gap in existing evidence.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Kelly Baker, CFO, Decision Maker)
economic_buyer.narrative: CFO Kelly Baker is named the Decision Maker via the relevant stakeholder (Decision_Maker_Identified__c = true), but she has never appeared on either matched call and has never replied to any outreach. Her position is known only second-hand: Jeannine Jenkins relayed on 28 May 2025 that 'our CFO has repeatedly asked why is our invoice process per AP team member so low... she doesn't understand why is our team only processing x amount of invoices.' No direct commercial or budget conversation with Kelly Baker has occurred.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence shows an escalation pattern: Chamika McLean was the sole contact on the 12 Mar and 18 Mar 2025 calls, then brought in Jeannine Jenkins for the 28 May 2025 deep-dive. Jeannine explicitly asked to 'reschedule another call and we could do a live demo with us' at the end of that call. Per Next_Step_History__c a workshop was tentatively targeted for the week of 23 Jun 2025, but the rep's own notes record 'Reached out to Chamika McLean - Controller on Vacation but no response on requested 2nd workshop,' and the process has not advanced at all in the 13 months since.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated informally that they want a system meeting 'at least 80% of requirements' versus Xflow's actual 5% match rate, need support for blanket-PO allocation across departments, multi-workflow (PO and non-PO) support, and minimal IT lift for D365 integration (Jeannine Jenkins asked directly 'how much IT resources... would an implementation require' on 28 May 2025). No formal BRD or scored criteria list has been produced or reconfirmed since.

items.name: Sequence call attempt to Matt Goyer
items.summary: Automated step 10 of the Emerging Enterprise outbound cadence (via Nooks dialer) placed a call to Matt Goyer; no notes or connect outcome recorded, so it's unclear if contact was made — still awaiting a live response.

day_summary.overall: A single dialer task fired today as part of the Emerging Enterprise outbound sequence, with a call attempt to Matt Goyer at Eversana marking step 10 of that cadence. No outcome, notes, or callback details were logged, so this reads as a routine automated touchpoint rather than a substantive conversation — the deal remains stalled at the prospecting/outreach stage, waiting on Matt to actually engage.

cro_panel.intro: A well-documented, buyer-quantified pain case from two real discovery calls sits alongside more than a year of silence from the champion, no economic-buyer engagement, and a close date that assumes a decision in 13 days at a stage that has never reached a demo.; The deal's fundamentals were promising when last touched but have gone cold

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Close date of 22 Jul 2026 is 15 days out with the deal still in Qualified stage and no demo, pricing, or contracting conversation begun.
CFO Kelly Baker (Decision Maker) has never appeared on a call; her frustration with AP inefficiency is referenced only second-hand.

blocks.read: Going quiet — engagement has dropped off.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: No external rival vendor has ever been named on any call or in Salesforce.

bullets.text: Champion Chamika McLean has gone silent for ~13 months since the 28 May 2025 call; no reply to any outreach is recorded.
Named Decision Maker (CFO Kelly Baker) has never joined a call or replied to outreach.

items: Send Chamika McLean and Jeannine Jenkins a direct email referencing their specific 5%-match and blanket-PO pain to test if the initiative is still live. (by 16 Jun)
If no reply, reach Kelly Baker directly, referencing her own quoted concern about AP team productivity. (by 23 Jun)
If reconnected, immediately propose the still-owed live demo/workshop with a solution consultant on Eversana's specific use cases. (by 8 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.detail: Close date (22 Jul 2026) sits weeks out while the deal is still Qualified with no demo, pricing, or contracting motion — a severe stage/timeline mismatch.
Close date already slipped 83 days once (Apr → Jul 2026), suggesting the date on file isn't grounded in real buyer activity.
No confirmed economic-buyer access — CFO has never engaged directly, only referenced second-hand.
Single-threaded on a champion who has been silent for 13 months, with no backup contact developed.
Rep's own next step frames the core question as whether this initiative is stalled, postponed, or cancelled — the deal may already be functionally dead.

win_position.text: Champion Chamika McLean, who drove the evaluation, has not responded to any outreach in ~13 months since the 28 May 2025 call.
Economic buyer CFO Kelly Baker has never appeared on a call; her involvement is referenced only second-hand — no real exec access.
No demo, pricing, or contracting conversation has begun despite a close date only weeks out (22 Jul 2026) — stage (Qualified) doesn't match the timeline.
MEDDPICC shows decision criteria, decision process, and paper process all as gaps — no vetted path to a decision.
Fit signal from the two real calls was strong: both Chamika and Jeannine independently voiced that AI was necessary to solve their AP problem — genuine buyer-voiced enthusiasm, just stale.
No external competitor is named, but the real threat is internal — the incumbent Xflow module plus a live risk the initiative is simply shelved (do-nothing).

deal_momentum.text: 407 days since the last buyer meeting (28 May 2025 'Zycus Eversana connect'); zero meetings logged in the 30/60/90-day windows.
No future meeting on the calendar — forward pipeline of engagement is empty.
Rep's current next step is literally to find out if the initiative is 'stalled, postponed or cancelled' — this is not forward motion.
Close date pushed 83 days (30 Apr 2026 → 22 Jul 2026) with no buyer activity accompanying the change.

The two substantive 2025 calls (Mar 18, May 28) were genuine two-way sessions with transcripts, not one-way rep sends — but that activity is now over a year stale and doesn't offset the current dark stretch.

customer_commitment.text: No security/procurement review, paper process, or reference requests recorded anywhere in the packet.
The one stakeholder who was driving buy-in (Chamika) has gone fully dark for 13 months — no buyer-side owner is currently active.
CFO Kelly Baker, the named decision maker, has never joined a call or replied to outreach — zero direct investment from the person who controls budget.
Early on, the champion proactively expanded the buying group on her own initiative — real, if now-stale, evidence of past buyer-side investment.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c = 6.0 (Moderate). Two discovery calls show genuine AI receptiveness: Chamika McLean called AI 'critical...pivotal' to business innovation (18 Mar 2025), and Jeannine Jenkins independently affirmed AI as 'a way to innovate' (28 May 2025). The moderate score reflects that this receptiveness has never been tested against a live demo and the initiative has since stalled for over a year.

Ownership flipped between reps twice in early June 2026 (administrative), but no buyer-facing content followed either change.

confidence_signals.summary: Two matched, transcribed discovery calls (18 Mar 2025, 28 May 2025) show real early engagement and pain articulation, but zero buyer-side contact of any kind (email reply, call answer, meeting) has been recorded since 5 Jun 2025 despite sustained rep outreach.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-03-18, moved Initial Interest -> Qualified on 2025-03-21 (476 days ago, still in Qualified 476 days later). Close date has been pushed four times (18 Sep 2025 -> 20 Feb 2026 -> 6 Feb 2026 -> 30 Apr 2026 -> 22 Jul 2026, last push 14 Apr 2026) with zero corresponding stage movement. Today is 15 days from the current close date with no evaluation milestone (demo, pricing, contract) yet reached.
evidence: Next step still reads '...determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled' (SF Next_Step__c, dated 2025-10-13)
north_star_verdict.headline: The Eversana AP-automation deal has been effectively dormant for over a year: the last genuine two-way buyer contact was Chamika McLean's 5 Jun 2025 email, and 13 months of steady rep outreach (calls, emails) has drawn no reply since. The 22 Jul 2026 close date (15 days out as of this sweep) is not credible against a deal still sitting in Qualified stage with no demo, pricing, or contracting conversation ever begun.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pull out of the current quarter / push close date materially; no realistic path to close by 22 Jul 2026 given the deal remains in Qualified stage with no live buyer engagement, demo, or pricing conversation on record.

flags: Re-open the thread with Chamika McLean directly — the 5%-match Xflow pain and CFO-level frustration she described on 28 May 2025 remain unresolved and Zycus has a strong technical fit; personal re-engagement is the highest-leverage move available.

Multi-thread beyond Chamika: bring in Matt Goyer (VP Procurement) or Justin Stenz (SVP Finance) as a second point of contact, since the deal currently rests on one AP-side champion who has gone quiet for over a year.
Re-engage CFO Kelly Baker directly instead of routing only through Chamika McLean; Jeannine Jenkins already confirmed CFO-level frustration with AP throughput but Kelly Baker has never joined a call.
Convert the 28 May 2025 pain data (5% vs 80% match rate, 4-5 touches per invoice, CFO frustration) into a written one-page business case now, before another sweep passes with zero fresh evidence.

Treat the 13-month silence as a re-engagement problem, not a nurture problem: escalate past Chamika McLean to Jeannine Jenkins or Justin Stenz with a fresh, dated ROI hook tied to their own stated 80% match-rate target.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal's biggest lever right now is reopening a stalled relationship at a higher level, not chasing the same unresponsive champion.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-side communication of any kind in the last 30-60 days; last substantive contact was 28 May 2025 (over 13 months ago).

competitive_position.summary: No competing vendor has been named in any source; Competitors__c is blank and neither matched Avoma call references a rival bidder. The only real 'competitor' is inertia: Eversana's own AP-automation initiative appears to have stalled internally, per the rep's own 13 Oct 2025 note asking whether it has been 'stalled, postponed, or cancelled,' with the incumbent Xflow/D365 AP module remaining the default status quo while no evaluation activity has occurred in over 13 months.

The deal's fundamentals were promising when last touched but have gone cold.

The champion, Chamika McLean, has not responded to any outreach since the 28 May 2025 call (~407 days ago), including a logged rep re-engagement attempt dated 13 Oct 2025.

The Next Step logged 13 Oct 2025 ("determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled") is itself ~9 months stale and unresolved.

That commitment has not translated into any further action: the promised demo/workshop and the buyer's promised availability were never followed through.

hard.ais_why:
• Two recorded discovery calls show clear interest in AI-driven AP automation; Zycus pitched cognitive OCR and AI invoice processing to fix Eversana's failing Xflow tool (5% PO-match versus 80% expected).
• Eversana's stakeholder explicitly framed AI as pivotal for innovation and reducing manual work, asking pointed questions about Microsoft Dynamics integration and IT effort needed for an AI procurement rollout.
• Qualified stage with two substantive 2025 calls; opportunity has stalled into nurture mode (Oct 13 next step questions if initiative is cancelled), limiting confidence despite strong AI receptiveness.
hard.next_step: 10/13/25 RH Invite Chamika to PLaN- determine if initiative is stalled, postponed or cancelled.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo / do-nothing (the AP automation initiative itself appears to have stalled internally)
